title: Ensure that the --token-auth-file parameter is not set

input_row.title: Ensure that the --token-auth-file parameter is not set
input_row.description: Do not use token based authentication.
input_row.rationale: The token-based authentication utilizes static tokens to authenticate requests to the apiserver. The tokens are stored in clear-text in a file on the apiserver, and cannot be revoked or rotated without restarting the apiserver. Hence, do not use static token- based authentication. Impact: You will have to configure and use alternate authentication mechanisms such as certificates. Static token based authentication could not be used.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --token-auth-file argument does not exist. Alternative Audit Method kubectl get pod -nkube-system -lcomponent=kube-apiserver -o=jsonpath='{range .items[ ]}{.spec.containers[ ].command} {"\n"}{end}' | grep '--token-auth-file' | grep -i false If the exit code is '1', then the control isn't present / failed
input_row.remediation: Follow the documentation and configure alternate mechanisms for authentication. Then, edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the master node and remove the --token-auth-file=<filename> parameter. Default Value: By default, --token-auth-file argument is not set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/authentication/#static-token-file 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/
input_row.profile_applicability: •  Level 1 - Master Node •  Level 2 - Master Node
input_row.impact: You will have to configure and use alternate authentication mechanisms such as certificates. Static token based authentication could not be used.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_token_auth_file_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the kube-apiserver Pod specification in the kube-system namespace using the Kubernetes API or kubectl.
- Specifically, inspect the command and args fields of the kube-apiserver container to check for the presence of the --token-auth-file flag.
- Example kubectl command:
  ```
  kubectl get pod -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.spec.containers[*].command}{"\n"}{end}'
  ```
  or
  ```
  kubectl get pod -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o json | jq -r '.items[].spec.containers[].command[]'
  ```
- Alternatively, check the static Pod manifest file (if accessible via the API) at /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml for the --token-auth-file argument.
- **Pass Criteria:** The --token-auth-file flag is NOT present in the kube-apiserver command or args.
- **Fail Criteria:** The --token-auth-file flag IS present in the kube-apiserver command or args, indicating static token authentication is enabled.
- No subjective review or host-level access is required; all information is available via Kubernetes API or kubectl. 

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.7.1 PDF
